flashcards.front: What are the five framework activities in software engineering?
flashcards.back: Communication, Planning, Modeling (Analysis & Design), Construction (Coding & Testing), and Deployment.

flashcards.front: What is the purpose of the Communication activity?
flashcards.back: To understand stakeholder needs, establish relationships, and gather requirements for the software project.

flashcards.front: What does the Planning activity involve?
flashcards.back: Estimating costs, defining tasks, scheduling milestones, and identifying risks for the project.

flashcards.front: What are the two main components of Modeling?
flashcards.back: Analysis (understanding requirements) and Design (creating the system architecture and structure).

flashcards.front: What does the Construction activity encompass?
flashcards.back: Code generation and testing to build the software system.

flashcards.front: What is the goal of the Deployment activity?
flashcards.back: To deliver the software to customers and ensure it functions properly in the operational environment.

flashcards.front: What is the purpose of software engineering principles?
flashcards.back: To guide framework activities, ensure quality, and provide systematic approaches to software development.

flashcards.front: What is requirement gathering in communication?
flashcards.back: The process of collecting and documenting what stakeholders expect from the software system.

flashcards.front: What is project scheduling in planning?
flashcards.back: Defining timelines, milestones, and sequence of tasks to complete the project on time.

flashcards.front: What is risk management in planning?
flashcards.back: Identifying potential problems that could occur and preparing mitigation strategies.
Creating representations of the system to understand requirements before design begins.
Creating blueprints and architecture that define how the system will be built.
Testing individual components or modules of code to ensure they function correctly.
Testing how different modules work together as a combined system.
Installing, configuring, and releasing the software for end-user operation.
